base_recipes.title: Berries & Cream French Toast Bake
base_recipes.description: A delightful and easy-to-make French toast bake with fresh mixed berries and a creamy custard center. Perfect for Easter brunch or any special breakfast.

steps.text: Cut the French bread into cubes.
Whip cream cheese with powdered sugar until smooth.
In a large dish, layer the bread cubes, dollops of sweetened cream cheese, and mixed berries.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and salt.
Pour the egg mixture over the layered bread and berries. Cover and refrigerate overnight.
Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
Sprinkle cinnamon sugar over the top and bake for 45 minutes, until the top is golden brown and crispy.
Serve warm with maple syrup.

base_recipes.title: Nigerian Rice & Chicken Stew
base_recipes.description: West African-style chicken stew in tomato and pepper sauce, served with boiled white rice and golden fried plantain.

steps.text: Wash and pat dry the chicken thighs, then season with salt and black pepper.
Heat olive oil and brown the chicken on both sides, then set the chicken aside.
Blend tomatoes, red bell pepper, habanero, and half the onion until smooth.
Sauté the remaining chopped onion in the pan until translucent.
Pour in the blended mixture and boil for 5 minutes.
Add chicken stock, bouillon powder, thyme, curry powder, and black pepper, then return chicken to the sauce.
Simmer for about 20 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked and the stew thickens; adjust salt to taste.
Boil the white rice in water until tender, then drain or steam-dry and fluff.
Fry diced ripe plantain in hot oil until golden brown, then drain.
Serve the chicken stew over white rice with fried plantain on the side.

base_recipes.title: Spinach One-Pot Pasta
base_recipes.description: This shockingly simple dish achieves success thanks to a small yet mighty powerhouse: Salty, umami-rich anchovies melt into the cooking oil, giving the dish subtle complexity and oomph. This pasta uses only a few ingredients and just one pot, making it perfect for quick dinners.

steps.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add the pasta and adjust the heat to maintain a gentle boil. Cook until just shy of al dente, about 1 minute less than the package instructions.
Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water.
Place the spinach in a large colander in the sink. Drain the pasta over the spinach. Drizzle with oil and stir to prevent the noodles from sticking.
Wipe out the pot and place it over medium-high heat. Carefully add 2 tablespoons olive oil and the anchovies (it might splatter) and stir until they start to dissolve, about 30 seconds.
Pour the pasta and spinach into the pot, add the cheese and red-pepper flakes, and stir until a glossy sauce forms and the spinach fully wilts, 1 to 2 minutes.
Squeeze a lemon half over everything. Taste and season as needed with more salt or lemon.

base_recipes.title: Guava Lemon Bars
base_recipes.description: These Guava Lemon Bars combine the zesty brightness of lemons with the tropical sweetness of guava, layered on a buttery shortbread crust for a delightful treat.

steps.text: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and adjust the oven rack to the middle position.
Line a 9x13-inch baking dish with parchment paper, leaving an overhang on the sides.
In a medium bowl, whisk together 2 cups of all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup of powdered sugar, and 1/4 teaspoon of salt.
Cut 1 cup (two sticks) of unsalted butter into small cubes and mix into the flour mixture using a pastry cutter or your fingers until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Press the mixture evenly into the prepared baking dish to form the crust.
Bake the crust for 20-25 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
While the crust is baking, combine 4 large eggs, 1 cup granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon lemon zest, and 1/3 cup fresh lemon juice in a large bowl.
Add 1/2 cup of guava puree and 1 tablespoon of all-purpose flour to the egg mixture and whisk until well combined.
Pour the guava lemon filling over the hot baked crust, spreading evenly with a spatula.
Bake for another 25-30 minutes, or until the filling is set and slightly golden around the edges.
Remove from the oven and let the bars cool completely in the pan on a wire rack.
Once cool, lift the bars out of the pan using the parchment overhang and cut into squares.
Dust with powdered sugar before serving, if desired.

adapted_recipe.title: Quick Berries & Cream French Toast Bake
adapted_recipe.summary: A simplified and faster version of the classic French toast bake with fresh berries and a creamy custard center, perfect for a busy day breakfast.
steps: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
Cut the French bread into cubes and place in a greased baking dish.
Whip cream cheese with powdered sugar until smooth and dollop over the bread cubes.
Add mixed berries on top of the bread and cream cheese.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, milk, vanilla extract, cinnamon, and salt.
Pour the egg mixture over the bread and berries, ensuring it's evenly coated.
Sprinkle with sugar and bake for 25-30 minutes until golden brown and set.
Serve warm with maple syrup.

adapted_recipe.summary: A fiery West African-style chicken stew infused with heat from habanero and cayenne, balanced with the richness of olive oil and the tang of lime, served with boiled white rice and golden fried plantain.

Fry diced ripe plantain in hot oil until golden brown, then drain.

adapted_recipe.summary: This quick and satisfying dish replaces traditional pasta with a delightful mix of sautéed spinach, anchovies, and creamy cheese, achieving a rich umami flavor without the carbs. Perfect for a keto-friendly dinner in just one pot!

Remove from heat and squeeze lemon juice over the dish. Taste and season with more salt if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with additional Parmesan if desired.
pairing_notes: Pair with avocado slices for added creaminess.
Serve alongside grilled chicken or fish for extra protein.

adapted_recipe.title: Reduced-Sugar Guava Lemon Bars
adapted_recipe.summary: These Guava Lemon Bars feature the zesty brightness of lemons and tropical guava, layered on a buttery shortbread crust, with reduced sweetness for a more balanced treat.
steps: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and adjust the oven rack to the middle position.
Line a 9x13-inch baking dish with parchment paper, leaving an overhang on the sides.
In a medium bowl, whisk together 2 cups of all-purpose flour, 5 tablespoons of powdered sugar, and ¼ teaspoon of salt.
Cut 1 cup (two sticks) of unsalted butter into small cubes and mix into the flour mixture using a pastry cutter or your fingers until mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Press the mixture evenly into the prepared baking dish to form the crust.
Bake the crust for 20-25 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
While the crust is baking, combine 4 large eggs, ½ cup granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon lemon zest, and 5 tablespoons fresh lemon juice in a large bowl.
Add ½ cup of guava puree and 1 tablespoon of all-purpose flour to the egg mixture and whisk until well combined.
Pour the guava lemon filling over the hot baked crust, spreading evenly with a spatula.
Bake for another 25-30 minutes, or until the filling is set and slightly golden around the edges.
Remove from the oven and let the bars cool completely in the pan on a wire rack.
Once cool, lift the bars out of the pan using the parchment overhang and cut into squares.

adapted_recipe.title: Light Guava Lemon Bars
adapted_recipe.summary: These Light Guava Lemon Bars maintain the zesty brightness of lemons and the tropical sweetness of guava, now with a lighter crust and filling for a refreshing treat.
steps: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and adjust the oven rack to the middle position.
Line a 9x13-inch baking dish with parchment paper, leaving an overhang on the sides.
In a medium bowl, whisk together 1 1/2 cups of all-purpose flour, 6 tablespoons of powdered sugar, and 1/4 teaspoon of salt.
Melt 1/2 cup of coconut oil and mix it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Press the mixture evenly into the prepared baking dish to form the crust.
Bake the crust for 15-20 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
While the crust is baking, combine 3 large eggs, 1/2 cup granulated sugar, 1 tablespoon lemon zest, and 5 tablespoons fresh lemon juice in a large bowl.
Add 6 tablespoons of guava puree and 1 tablespoon of all-purpose flour to the egg mixture and whisk until well combined.
Pour the guava lemon filling over the hot baked crust, spreading evenly with a spatula.
Bake for another 20-25 minutes, or until the filling is set and slightly golden around the edges.
Remove from the oven and let the bars cool completely in the pan on a wire rack.
Once cool, lift the bars out of the pan using the parchment overhang and cut into squares.
